subject: Databases
title: NOT NULL and Defaults
description: One-minute skill: NOT NULL and Defaults.

hook.text: A user signs up but skips the bio field. Should it be NULL or an empty string? NOT NULL with a default decides.

buildup.text: NOT NULL means the column must always have a value. The database rejects inserts that leave it empty.

discovery.text: DEFAULT provides a fallback: created_at DEFAULT NOW(), status DEFAULT 'active'. No explicit value needed.

twist.text: NULLs complicate queries. WHERE name != 'Ada' excludes NULL rows. Three-valued logic trips up even senior devs.

climax.text: Use NOT NULL by default. Allow NULL only when the absence of a value has real meaning.

punchline.text: Default to NOT NULL. Allow NULL only when absence means something.

quiz.question: Why can NULL values complicate SQL queries?
options: SQL uses three-valued logic where NULL comparisons behave unexpectedly
NULL values take up more disk space
NULLs prevent the table from being indexed
